wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the left-front (7 o'clock)

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Wind from the left-front. A stable disc thrown with a hyzer should hold its line well, accounting for the leftward drift.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An understable disc can be used for a controlled right-fade. Be mindful of the wind's influence.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: This angle requires an overstable disc for a reliable right fade against the wind.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: A stable disc thrown with a slight anhyzer will allow the wind to carry it left. Aim carefully.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the left-front (7 o'clock)

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Similar to Hole 1 [yellow], wind from the left-front. A stable disc with a hyzer is recommended to counter the drift.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An understable disc can be used for a controlled right-fade. Keep the wind's leftward push in mind.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: An overstable disc is needed for a solid right fade against the wind.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: With wind pushing left, a stable disc thrown with anhyzer can work. Aim carefully to avoid overshooting left.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the right-front (10 o'clock)

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Wind from the right-front. An understable disc will be pushed left. Use a slight hyzer to counter this and get a right fade.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An overstable disc is needed to fight the wind and fade right. Aim to the left of the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: Wind from the right is pushing left. A stable to overstable disc will help achieve the required right fade.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: An understable disc can be thrown for a controlled fade left, working with the wind.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the left-front (4 o'clock)

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: A stable disc thrown with a slight hyzer should hold a line towards the basket. Wind is pushing from the left-front, so expect some drift. Keep it clean.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An understable disc will fight this wind and potentially drift right. Consider a stable disc for a more predictable flight path.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH: This is a tricky angle with wind pushing left. An overstable disc is needed to fade reliably right, fighting the wind and the natural fade.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: With wind pushing left, a stable disc is a good option to go straight or fade left. Be careful not to throw too wide left, as the wind will carry it further.

wind_info.description: Moderate headwind (12 o-clock)

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: A moderate headwind requires an overstable disc to prevent it from turning over. Throw with a hyzer for maximum stability.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An overstable disc is also recommended for forehands into this headwind. Aim to keep it low and straight.

fh.shot_strategy: LHFH: An overstable disc will hold its line well into the headwind. Focus on a powerful, clean release.
